prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. Verily, the alchemists of the Cloud Citadel have transmuted naught but ethereal vapours into 2.3 million golden florins this quarter, though no man saw them forge a single coin!  

2. The scroll of subscriptions grew by 420 souls, yet these new vassals dwell not in any shire I can chart—only in the bewitched glass rectangles the scribes stare into whilst muttering of "user engagement."  

3. Lo, the Oracle of Burn Rate did foretell that our coffers shall last seven and a half moons, provided the goblins of Customer Acquisition Costs cease their infernal toll-gathering at the digital crossroads.  

4. The Baron of Product hath unveiled a "feature enhancement" that allows phantom couriers to deliver missives through the aether, though I fear it works not for carrier pigeons, much to the pigeon-master’s despair.
